channel.title: World
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from World, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Australia politics live: Liberals wait for leadership spill after Angus Taylor’s resignation
item.description: Sussan Ley could face a party room vote as early as today. Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast McCar...

item.title: Trump officials face tense congressional hearings; president hosts Israeli prime minister Netanyahu – US politics live
item.description: US attorney general Pam Bondi and veteran affairs secretary Doug Collins clash with House Democrats; Trump hosts Netanyahu amid Iran tensions Transpor...

item.title: UK law firms consider action on behalf of women who developed brain tumours after using contraceptive
item.description: Multiple studies show that women who take Depo-Provera have much higher risk of developing meningiomas UK law firms are considering legal action on be...

item.title: Trump says he is still seeking Iran anti-nuclear deal after Netanyahu meeting
item.description: Israeli leader was expected to advocate for more forceful US intervention during sixth visit to current White House Donald Trump has said that he is s...

item.title: Canadian police identify suspect in school massacre that left nine dead
item.description: A teacher and five students among those killed in attack in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, on Tuesday Canadian police have identified the suspect wh...

item.title: Andrew should be interviewed by police, former PM Gordon Brown says
item.description: Gordon Brown said police should "urgently" re-examine their investigation into Epstein's victims possibly being trafficked in and out of the UK.

item.title: Mouat bounces back as men's curling favourites GB start with win
item.description: Bruce Mouat recovers from his Winter Olympics curling mixed doubles disappointment in some style, leading his Team GB men's rink to a 9-4 victory in t...

item.title: Dawson's Creek star James Van Der Beek dies aged 48
item.description: The father of six starred in multiple popular shows and films in the late 1990s and early 2000s, including Dawson's Creek and Varsity Blues.

item.title: Epstein survivor calls on Palace to search Andrew's files
item.description: Juliette Bryant has called on the Palace to "act" on revelations about Andrew's relationship with Epstein.

item.title: Al Fayed victims say cases should be investigated as human trafficking
item.description: Police are investigating more than five people who may have facilitated Al Fayed in his alleged abuse.
